Name: WUQuickHealth
Description: 30-second triage snapshot: services, disk space, pending reboots, recent failures with HRESULT translation, cache size, and last update date. Routes tech to the correct follow-up scriptlet.

Param1.Name: DaysBack
Param1.Description: How many days of WU history to scan (default: 30)

Notes: v2.0: Complete rewrite replacing DiagnoseWindowsUpdate. Checks 4 core services (incl. UsoSvc), disk space (5/20 GB thresholds), 3 reboot signals (incl. Session Manager), WU history with 20-entry HRESULT translation table, SoftwareDistribution cache size, and last-success date. Read-only diagnostic.

Name: WUPolicyAudit
Description: Read-only audit of GPO, MDM/Intune, and UX-level policy settings governing Windows Update: WSUS routing, auto-update behavior, deferral periods, pause state, active hours, Delivery Optimization, target group, and split-brain GPO/MDM conflict detection with MDMWinsOverGP resolution.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. Audits 3 registry layers: GPO (HKLM Policies WindowsUpdate), MDM (PolicyManager current device Update), and UX (WindowsUpdate UX Settings). Includes WSUS TCP reachability (3s timeout), ExcludeWUDriversInQualityUpdate from both GPO and MDM, and MDMWinsOverGP split-brain detection.

Name: WUNetworkCheck
Description: Tests whether the machine can reach Microsoft Update infrastructure. Checks DNS resolution, HTTPS connectivity (TCP 443), WinHTTP proxy, system proxy registry, PAC/WPAD auto-config, VPN adapter detection, and metered connection status.
Notes: Read-only network diagnostic. Tests 4 Microsoft Update endpoints + WSUS (if configured). Uses TcpClient with 5s timeout (not Invoke-WebRequest). Detects WinHTTP proxy via netsh, system proxy via HKLM registry, PAC/WPAD via AutoConfigURL and DefaultConnectionSettings blob. VPN detection via Get-NetAdapter keyword matching. Metered detection via DefaultMediaCost registry + Get-NetConnectionProfile. Does not check TLS/certs/clock (WU004 scope).

Name: DEFStatusTriage
Description: 30-second triage: Security Center AV products with bitmask decoding, Defender running mode, RTP, definitions, services, MDE sensor, and signal gap analysis. Routes tech to the correct follow-up scriptlet.
Notes: v2.0: Complete rewrite. Replaces DiagnoseDefenderSensor. Added Security Center bitmask decoding, AMRunningMode passive detection, ghost registration detection, MDE Sense check, signal gap analysis, NEXT footer routing. Read-only diagnostic.

Name: DEFDefinitionHealth
Description: Read-only diagnostic of the Defender definition update pipeline: signature age (hourly precision for AV, Antispyware, NIS), update source configuration (GPO FallbackOrder, ForceUpdateFromMU, polling interval), CDN connectivity (definitionupdates.microsoft.com, go.microsoft.com), recent update failure events with HRESULT translation, and scheduled task health (existence, state, last run result).
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. Checks 3 signature types with hourly precision, GPO update source configuration including Co-Management sabotage detection, TCP 443 to Microsoft CDN endpoints (3s timeout), last 48h of Defender operational event log for Event 2001/2003 failures, and 4 Windows Defender scheduled tasks. Does not duplicate WSUS reachability (see WU002).

Name: DEFThirdPartyAV
Description: Detects third-party AV conflicts, ghost Security Center registrations, uninstalled AV remnants (registry, services, filter drivers), and legacy DisableAntiSpyware/DisableAntiVirus policy overrides.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. 4 check groups: (1) Security Center deep enumeration with bitmask decode, exe path validation, instance GUID reporting, (2) AV remnant scan across 10 vendors (registry paths, leftover services, leftover filter drivers in System32\drivers), (3) Ghost registration analysis cross-referencing Security Center with Defender AMRunningMode, (4) Defender policy overrides (DisableAntiSpyware, DisableAntiVirus) at GPO and MDM paths with entanglement detection. Does not perform remediation (DEF008 scope).

Name: WingetUpgradeSystemSilent
Description: Runs winget upgrade --all as SYSTEM to silently update all machine-wide installed applications (Program Files). No UAC prompt. Skips user-scoped apps — use WingetUpgradeUserApps for those.
Notes: v1.2: Runs as SYSTEM to avoid UAC for machine-wide apps. Uses -AllUsers on Get-AppxPackage. Added --disable-interactivity.

Name: WingetUpgradeUserApps
Description: Runs winget upgrade --all --scope user as the logged-on user to update user-scoped applications installed in AppData (Spotify, Discord, VS Code user installs, etc.). Complements WingetUpgradeAllSilent which handles machine-wide apps.
Notes: Runs as logged-on user via Invoke-AsUser. Uses --scope user to only touch AppData installs. No UAC prompt needed.

Name: IntuneForceComplianceCheck
Description: Triggers a forced Intune compliance evaluation by invoking the Intune Management Extension agent with the synccompliance URI.
Script:
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

$imePath = 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Intune Management Extension\Microsoft.Management.Services.IntuneWindowsAgent.exe'

if (-not (Test-Path -Path $imePath)) {
    Write-Output ''
    Write-Output '[ERR] Intune Management Extension not found.'
    Write-Output "      Expected at: $imePath"
    Write-Output '      This device may not be Intune-enrolled.'
    Write-Output ''
    return
}

Write-Output ''
Write-Output '=== Intune Compliance Sync ==='
Write-Output ''

try {
    Start-Process -FilePath $imePath -ArgumentList 'intunemanagementextension://synccompliance'
    Write-Output '[OK] Compliance sync triggered successfully.'
    Write-Output '     The device will evaluate compliance policies shortly.'
}
catch {
    Write-Output "[ERR] Failed to trigger compliance sync: $($_.Exception.Message)"
}

Write-Output ''
Notes: Runs as SYSTEM. The IME agent handles the compliance evaluation internally.

Name: BLStatusSnapshot
Description: Triage snapshot: volume encryption status with ghost-state detection, OS drive letter validation, last BitLocker event, and BDESVC service health. Routes tech to the correct follow-up scriptlet.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. Primary API is Get-BitLockerVolume with WMI Win32_EncryptableVolume fallback. Ghost state detection (encrypted + protection off) is a critical finding.

Name: BLTpmHealth
Description: Read-only diagnostic of the Trusted Platform Module (TPM): presence and state (TpmPresent, TpmReady, TpmEnabled, TpmActivated, TpmOwned with auto-provisioning check), specification version (1.2 vs 2.0 via Win32_Tpm WMI), manufacturer firmware vulnerability scanning (Infineon ROCA CVE-2017-15361, STMicro TPM-FAIL CVE-2019-16863), lockout state with estimated heal time calculation, and attestation/provisioning readiness (TBS service, owner auth retention level).
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. Uses Get-Tpm cmdlet with tpmtool.exe fallback when PowerShell module is unavailable. Checks 5 areas: TPM presence/state, TPM spec version (via Win32_Tpm WMI SpecVersion), firmware vulnerability scanning against known CVEs, lockout state with heal time calculation, and provisioning readiness (TBS service, auto-provisioning, owner auth level). Early exit if TPM not present. Cannot fix BIOS-level TPM issues -- script clearly says when the tech needs to enter BIOS setup.

Name: BLHardwarePrereqs
Description: Validates hardware and firmware prerequisites for BitLocker: UEFI vs Legacy BIOS, Secure Boot, GPT vs MBR, system partition, Modern Standby, and OEM-specific quirk detection.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. 6 check groups: (1) boot mode UEFI/Legacy via Confirm-SecureBootUEFI with bcdedit fallback, (2) Secure Boot status with registry cross-reference, (3) disk partition scheme GPT/MBR via Get-Disk with CIM fallback, (4) system partition validation (EFI partition, size, FAT32 format), (5) Modern Standby via powercfg /a + CsEnabled registry + PlatformAoAcOverride override, (6) OEM identification with Dell/Lenovo/HP quirk advisories. Clearly distinguishes BIOS-level fixes from PowerShell-fixable issues.

Name: FWStatusTriage
Description: Triage snapshot: firewall profile status with active adapter correlation, Security Center cross-reference for ghost detection and Intune desync, MpsSvc service health, and network adapter inventory. Routes tech to the correct follow-up scriptlet.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. Cross-references Get-NetFirewallProfile with Get-NetConnectionProfile to determine which profiles are actively filtering. Compares against Security Center WMI to detect Intune compliance desync.

Name: FWPolicyConflict
Description: Read-only diagnostic that reads firewall settings from all three policy sources (Local registry, GPO registry, MDM registry) and displays them side-by-side for each profile (Domain, Private, Public). Detects EnableFirewall=0 from any source, checks MDMWinsOverGP conflict resolution status with confirmation key validation, and identifies orphaned GPO tattooing on non-domain-joined machines.
Notes: Registry-read-only diagnostic. Reads from 3 sources: Local (CurrentControlSet\SharedAccess\Parameters\FirewallPolicy), GPO (SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\WindowsFirewall), MDM (SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\current\device\Firewall). Checks MDMWinsOverGP with ProviderSet/WinningProvider confirmation. Detects orphaned GPO keys on non-domain-joined machines. Does not query live firewall state (that is FW001's job). Does not remediate (that is FW006's job).

Name: FWThirdParty
Description: Deep enumeration of Security Center FirewallProduct entries with full productState bitmask decode, registry scan of 14 known third-party firewall vendors (Uninstall keys, install paths, vendor-specific registry), Managed by Vendor state detection via local EnableFirewall per-profile, ghost registration cross-reference analysis with confidence scoring, and WFP filter driver detection via fltmc.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. Detects active third-party firewalls and ghost registrations (orphaned WMI entries from uninstalled products). 14 vendors: Symantec, McAfee/Trellix, Sophos, ESET, Comodo, ZoneAlarm, GlassWire, TinyWall, Kaspersky, Norton, Bitdefender, F-Secure/WithSecure, Trend Micro, CrowdStrike. Uses fltmc for WFP filter driver detection. Does not remediate ghosts (FW006 scope). Does not read GPO/MDM policies (FW002 scope).

Name: WUTlsCertCheck
Description: Verifies cryptographic and time-keeping prerequisites for Windows Update: TLS 1.2 Schannel/WinHTTP configuration, .NET Framework strong crypto defaults, system clock drift via w32tm, Microsoft root certificate presence and validity, and FIPS mode status.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. Checks 6 areas: Schannel TLS 1.2 (Client/Server subkeys + legacy protocol detection), .NET Framework SchUseStrongCrypto and SystemDefaultTlsVersions (64-bit + WOW6432Node), WinHTTP DefaultSecureProtocols bitmask, system clock offset via w32tm stripchart, Microsoft Root CA 2011 + ECC 2017 certificate validation, and FIPS algorithm policy. Does not check network connectivity (WU003 scope) or component health (WU005 scope).

Name: WUComponentHealth
Description: Checks the Windows servicing stack health: DISM /CheckHealth for corruption flags, CBS.log tail analysis for component store HRESULTs, last SFC /scannow result extraction, and DISM /AnalyzeComponentStore for sizing and cleanup recommendation. Read-only diagnostic.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. 4 check groups: DISM health (Repair-WindowsImage -CheckHealth with dism.exe fallback, reports ImageHealthState enum, notes registry-only limitation), CBS.log analysis (tail 200 lines, 8 corruption HRESULTs from research: 0x80073712/0x800F081F/0x800F0831/0x800736CC critical, 0x800F0823/0x800F0982/0x8007000D/0x80070020 warning, plus Store corruption and Exec Error text markers), last SFC result (5000 line scan depth, [SR] tag parsing, 4 outcome strings), component store sizing (dism.exe /AnalyzeComponentStore stdout regex, 6 metrics including last cleanup date). Does not check services (WU001), policies (WU002), network (WU003), TLS (WU004), or event timeline (WU006). Does not run SFC or DISM repairs (WU010).

Name: WUEventTimeline
DisplayName: WU Event Log Timeline & HRESULT Deep Dive
Description: Pulls WU-related events from WindowsUpdateClient/Operational, BITS-Client/Operational, and System log (WU sources + SCM crash events for WU services), merges into a chronological timeline (capped at 50 most recent), extracts HRESULTs from failure events and translates them via a 25-entry map with actionable routing to WU002-WU009. Read-only diagnostic.

Param1.Name: DaysBack
Param1.Description: How many days of event history to pull.

Notes: Read-only diagnostic. 3 check groups: (1) Event summary statistics (total/success/failure/info counts, most common error code). (2) Chronological timeline of up to 50 most recent events from 3 sources: WindowsUpdateClient/Operational (IDs 19/20/21/25/26/31/41/42/43/44), BITS-Client/Operational (IDs 3/4/5/59/60/64), System log filtered to Microsoft-Windows-WindowsUpdateClient provider and Service Control Manager IDs 7031/7034/7036/7043 for WU services (wuauserv/BITS/TrustedInstaller/UsoSvc). (3) HRESULT summary grouped by error code with occurrence count, plain-English translation from 25-entry embedded map, and scriptlet routing. Does not duplicate WU001 Check 4 (COM API history) or WU005 Check 2-3 (CBS.log parsing).

Name: DEFRealtimeProtection
Description: Deep dive into RTP state with sub-component breakdown and source attribution (GPO/MDM/local), Tamper Protection diagnostics (value decode, source, TPExclusions), MsMpEng.exe process health (memory/CPU thresholds), exclusion audit with dangerous pattern detection, and ASR rule inventory with high-disruption flagging.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. 5 check groups: RTP sub-component breakdown (RTP, OnAccess, BehaviorMonitor, IOAV) with GPO/MDM/local source attribution, Tamper Protection registry decode (TamperProtection 5/4/0, TamperProtectionSource, TPExclusions, ManagedDefenderProductType), MsMpEng.exe process health (working set 500MB/1GB thresholds, CPU 2-second sampling with 10%/30% thresholds), exclusion audit via Get-MpPreference (11 dangerous path patterns including root drives, system folders, user-context variable traps + 10 dangerous extension types), ASR rule inventory (15-rule GUID-to-name table with 5 high-disruption rule flagging). Does not check Security Center bitmask (DEF001), definition health (DEF002), third-party AV remnants (DEF003), or full policy comparison (DEF005).

Name: DEFPolicyConflict
Description: Side-by-side comparison of Defender settings across GPO, MDM/Intune, and local preferences. Detects policy conflicts where GPO and MDM disagree, identifies dangerous GPO overrides that disable protection, checks ForceDefenderPassiveMode with third-party AV cross-reference, and validates MDMWinsOverGP (which does NOT apply to Defender CSP). Read-only diagnostic.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. 5 check groups: (1) Core protection settings side-by-side across 3 sources (GPO/MDM/Local) for 9 settings (RTP, BehaviorMonitor, IOAV, MAPS, SampleSubmission, NetworkProtection, PUA, ControlledFolderAccess, ScanScheduleDay) with conflict highlighting and dangerous pattern detection (GPO disabling critical protection). (2) Exclusion source comparison (GPO/MDM/Local counts per type + DisableLocalAdminMerge check). (3) ASR rule source comparison (GPO vs effective action conflicts). (4) ForceDefenderPassiveMode with SecurityCenter2 third-party AV cross-reference. (5) MDMWinsOverGP assessment -- documents that it does NOT apply to Defender CSP settings. Does not check definition update sources (DEF002), exclusion dangerous patterns (DEF004), ASR GUID-to-name mapping (DEF004), tamper protection (DEF004), DisableAntiSpyware/DisableAntiVirus (DEF003), or services/MDE sensor (DEF001).

Name: DEFPlatformVersion
Description: Validates Defender platform (MoCAMP), engine, and NIS engine versions against known-good baselines; decodes update channel/ring policy across GPO/MDM/local; queries platform update events with HRESULT translation; checks for orphaned MoCAMP update locks; correlates platform staleness with WSUS pinning.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. 5 check groups: (1) Component version comparison (platform, engine, NIS) against hardcoded baselines with N-2 deprecation awareness (warn at 4.18.26010.0, critical at 4.18.25100.0). (2) Update channel/ring decode (PlatformUpdatesChannel, EngineUpdatesChannel) across GPO/MDM/Local with 0-6 integer-to-channel mapping and delayed ring detection. (3) Platform update event log queries (Event 2002 success, Events 2007/5100/5101 deprecation warnings, Events 2001/2003 filtered for platform-specific failures) with 10-entry HRESULT translation table. (4) MoCAMP mechanism health (orphaned MoCAMPUpdateStarted lock detection, platform staging directory inspection with version comparison, wuauserv/BITS service state). (5) WSUS version pinning correlation (WSUS server detection + platform staleness cross-reference). Does not check definition/signature age (DEF002), services/Security Center/running mode (DEF001), third-party AV/ghost registrations (DEF003), RTP/tamper/exclusions/ASR (DEF004), or protection policy conflicts (DEF005).

Name: BLIntunePolicy
Description: Bridges the gap between what Intune expects and what the machine is configured for. Parses dsregcmd /status for MDM enrollment state, reads BitLocker CSP from PolicyManager registry, cross-references policy settings against hardware capabilities, scans IME logs for BitLocker entries, and validates MDM enrollment health via scheduled tasks and device certificate.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. 5 check groups: dsregcmd /status parsing for join state (AzureAdJoined/DomainJoined/WorkplaceJoined, MdmUrl, DeviceId with 5 derived states), BitLocker CSP registry at PolicyManager/current/device/BitLocker (RequireDeviceEncryption, EncryptionMethodByDriveType XML decode for OS/Fixed/Removable ciphers, AllowStandardUserEncryption, AllowWarningForOtherDiskEncryption, SystemDrivesRequireStartupAuthentication PIN detection), policy-hardware cross-reference (XTS vs OS build, TPM+PIN vs silent, cipher mismatch with encrypted volume), IME log scan (last 5000 lines for BitLocker keyword, 20 most recent matches displayed), MDM enrollment health (EnterpriseMgmt scheduled tasks with last run/result, MDM device certificate expiration from Cert:\LocalMachine\My). Does not check volume encryption status (BL001), TPM health (BL002), hardware prereqs (BL003), escrow (BL005), or GPO conflicts (BL006).

Name: BLEscrowCheck
Description: Traces the recovery key escrow pipeline: policy requirements, AAD device registration and identity health (zombie PRT detection), escrow event history with HRESULT translation, cloud endpoint connectivity, recovery key protector GUID-to-event cross-reference, and WinRE status. Read-only diagnostic.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. 6 check groups: (1) Escrow policy requirements from FVE registry (OSRequireActiveDirectoryBackup gate, FDVRequireActiveDirectoryBackup, OSActiveDirectoryInfoToStore). (2) AAD device registration via dsregcmd /status parsing for AzureAdPrt, DeviceAuthStatus (zombie state detection), TpmProtected, TenantId -- complementary to BL004 which reads enrollment fields. (3) Escrow event history 7-day window for Event IDs 845 (success), 846 (failure with 6-code HRESULT table), 851 (silent encryption failed), 858 (rotation failed), 778 (rollback). Includes 200-key limit hint on repeated failures. (4) Escrow endpoint connectivity TCP 443 to login.microsoftonline.com, enterpriseregistration.windows.net, device.login.microsoftonline.com with 3-second timeout. (5) Recovery key protector status with GUID extraction and Event 845 cross-reference for escrow confirmation. (6) Lightweight WinRE check via reagentc /info. Does not duplicate BL001 volume status, BL002 TPM diagnostics, BL003 hardware prereqs, or BL004 MDM enrollment/CSP settings.

Name: BLPolicyConflict
DisplayName: BitLocker Group Policy vs MDM Conflict Detection
Description: Detects group policy vs MDM conflicts that silently block BitLocker encryption. Reads GPO settings from HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE and MDM settings from PolicyManager, compares encryption method, startup authentication, recovery password, and PIN length. Checks MDMWinsOverGP assessment with BitLocker CSP limitation warning. Detects orphaned (tattooed) GPO settings from previous domain membership.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. 5 check groups: (1) GPO-delivered BitLocker settings from FVE registry with cipher/TPM usage decode tables and legacy PCR validation subkey detection. (2) MDM-delivered BitLocker settings from PolicyManager with XML parsing for EncryptionMethodByDriveType and SystemDrivesRequireStartupAuthentication. (3) Conflict detection comparing encryption method, startup PIN, AD backup deadlock, and minimum PIN length between GPO and MDM. (4) MDMWinsOverGP assessment at PolicyManager/current/device/ControlPolicyConflict with BitLocker CSP limitation warning (MDMWinsOverGP does NOT fully apply to BitLocker CSP settings). (5) Orphaned GPO settings detection via domain-join state check with specific dangerous patterns (legacy PCR profiles, AD backup on non-domain device, orphaned PIN requirement).

Name: FWServiceHealth
Description: Deep plumbing check when obvious causes (policy, third-party) have been ruled out. Validates the full RpcSs->BFE->MpsSvc dependency chain with exit codes, checks WFP engine responsiveness via netsh, audits firewall log configuration and recent DROP activity, validates MpsSvc SDDL for tampering (ZeroAccess detection), and scans firewall event logs for service failure events.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. 5 check groups: service dependency chain (RpcSs/BFE/MpsSvc status, start type, Win32 exit codes with cascade failure explanation), WFP state (netsh wfp show state to temp file, responsiveness check, file size indicator), firewall log config (LogBlocked/LogAllowed per profile via Get-NetFirewallProfile, log file existence/size, tail 50 lines for DROP and INFO-EVENTS-LOST detection), service security descriptor (sc.exe sdshow MpsSvc with SY and BA ACE validation for malware tampering), firewall event log (WFAS log ID 2003, System log IDs 5027/5028/5030/5035/5037, last 24h). Does not check profile enabled/disabled state (FW001), policy conflicts (FW002), third-party products (FW003), or rule diagnostics (FW005).

Name: FWRuleDiagnostic
Description: Diagnoses firewall rule bloat and corruption: total rule count with CIM/registry fallback, per-profile distribution, 6-field duplicate detection, orphaned application path validation (including %LocalAppData% user profile expansion), registry store size measurement against 14 MB MpsSvc limit, enabled vs disabled ratio, and Event ID 4953 rule corruption detection. Read-only diagnostic.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. 7 check groups: (1) Total rule count via CIM MSFT_NetFirewallRule with 30-second timeout, falling back to direct registry count at HKLM FirewallRules if CIM hangs. Thresholds: <500 OK, 500-3000 info, 3001-10000 warn, >10000 critical. (2) Per-profile distribution from registry pipe-delimited Profile= field (Domain/Private/Public/All), flags >70% concentration or >5000 in single profile. (3) Duplicate rule detection using 6-field fingerprint (Name+Direction+Action+Program+Protocol+LocalPort) from registry parsing with hash-based grouping, reports top 5 offenders. (4) Orphaned application path validation with SYSTEM-context %LocalAppData%/%APPDATA%/%USERPROFILE% expansion against all C:\Users profiles, plus absolute user path validation. (5) Rule store size measurement of FirewallRules registry key (Unicode byte calculation), flags >10 MB approaching 14 MB MpsSvc payload limit. (6) Enabled vs disabled rule ratio from Active= field, flags >5000 enabled rules. (7) Event ID 4953 (rule ignored because unparseable) from Security log, 7-day window. Does not duplicate FW001 profile status, FW002 policy reads, FW003 third-party detection, or FW004 service health/event IDs (5027/5028/5030/5035/5037).
